concerns.analysis: There are missing links in the extracted JSON where the anchor text is present but the hyperlink/URL is gone. For example, on pages 189 and 191, the word 'Rulemaking' serves as a clickable hyperlink in the source PDF pointing to another section of the document. In the JSON output, this text is extracted as an isolated plain text paragraph with no corresponding URL or anchor element.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The text 'Rulemaking' in sentences like 'The fiscal analysis can be found in the Rulemaking section...'

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains several meaningful line and bubble charts in the 'Kingfishes Fishery Management Plan' section. In the HTML output, the images provided for Figures 6, 10, and 12 are incorrect. Instead of the actual charts from the source document, the HTML embeds screenshots of the source page's footer and navigation buttons. Consequently, the actual charts representing the data for these figures are entirely missing from the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Line chart for "Figure 6. Annual proportion of adults (southern kingfish) greater than or equal to the length at 50% maturity..."
concerns.output_quote: The image provided for Figure 6 captures the page footer instead of the chart (``). The actual chart is missing.

concerns.analysis: The table on page 185 (a continuation of the 'Eligibility Pool Board Meeting Summary' from page 184) has corrupted structure. Because the 'INCOMP.' column is visually blank for these rows in the source PDF, the extraction incorrectly assigned a `num_columns: 2` span to the data cells in the adjacent 'TOTAL' column (e.g., the cell with '21' at row 53). This inappropriately merges the columns rather than maintaining an empty cell in the 'INCOMP.' column, corrupting the table's grid structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table rows under headers TOTAL and INCOMP. (e.g., the row for 10/4/2006 has '21' under TOTAL and a blank space under INCOMP.)

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains multiple Tables of Contents that were incorrectly retained in the extraction with their dotted leaders and meaningless page number suffixes. In HTML, page numbers are irrelevant, and retaining a printed TOC structure (especially inside `<ol>` or `<ul>` list elements or paragraphs) results in dead navigation references for screen readers. This occurs on pages 98, 197, and 269.

On page 24, the source PDF contains a scanned image of a formal letter. The HTML output embeds a full-page image of this letter while also providing the fully extracted text of the letter immediately below it. This forces the user to see the same content twice (once as an image and once as structured HTML), which constitutes visual duplication.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Letter text starting with "March 1, 2019 Gregg Waugh..."
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML contains both a full-page image of the letter (``) and the extracted text of the letter (`

March 1, 2019

Gregg Waugh...`).

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 4 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=4. Examples: pages 15-16: 5 cols (roll call vote...); pages 51-52: 7 cols (december...); pages 137-139: 2 cols (to:, n.c. marine fisheries commission...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (4 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=4. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Missing images: 12 of 73 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=73, html_img_count=61, image_count_ratio=1.9589041095890412. Some source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 16 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=16. Examples: p1: "A repeating pattern of light blue, stylized fish s"; p20: "A vertical dark blue band on the left features a r"; p23: "A vertical design featuring a repeating pattern of". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 77 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=77, examples=['https://files.nc.gov/ncdeq/051420-mfc-meeting/03-ChairmanRep', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (433): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=433, pages_affected=344/350. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 20 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://www.ncsbe.gov/Ethics/Education; https://EFILE.ncsbe.gov/; https://ncdenrits.webex.com/ncdenrits/onstage/g.php?MTID=ea8608d0638d06136715b7a; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/mfc-proposed-rules.*; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/shellfish-closure-maps.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=20. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p202→p203: list item ends “…other swimming area where people use the”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 3 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "A. North Carolina’s Shellfish Lease Program ................"; "A. Previous Legislative Studies ............................"; "A. Recommendation #1: Regulatory Reform ...................."
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=3. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 85 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (80 truncated mid-sentence, 5 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "... in horizontal rows against a blue background that"; p20: "...ng pattern of light blue stylized fish silhouettes"; p21: "...ng an illustration of a stylized fish leaping over"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=85 (truncated=80, placeholder=5, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

In the proposed rules sections (e.g., pages 202-205, 218-232, 261-267), the source PDF uses underline formatting to denote new additions to the regulatory text. The output JSON fails to capture this underline formatting, which represents a loss of structural emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The text is underlined in the PDF: "(b) A swimming advisory shall be issued by the Division and at least one sign shall be posted at the public access to swimming waters that have been impacted by a wastewater system failure. The sign for waters impacted by a wastewater spill shall state the following:"
concerns.output_quote: "(b) A swimming advisory shall be issued by the Division and at least one sign shall be posted at the public access to swimming waters that have been impacted by a wastewater system failure. The sign for waters impacted by a wastewater spill shall state the following:"

concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages in first/last position

concerns.description: Degenerate images detected: 4 images with extreme aspect ratio or tiny dimensions
Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 2 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=2. Examples: 6 item(s): "† The schedule assumes no rulemaking is required t"; 1 item(s): "*Fisheries Director no longer has authority to ope". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.
